title: How Water Is Becoming Pakistan’s Nuclear Threshold
gist: Pakistan has escalated official rhetoric around the Indus Waters Treaty, with its National Security Committee explicitly warning that any Indian move to stop or divert Pakistan's water would be treated as an act of war met with the 'complete spectrum' of national power — an implicit nuclear threat.

insights.claim: Pakistan's National Security Committee has stated that any attempt to stop or divert the flow of water belonging to Pakistan will be considered an Act of War and responded to with the 'full force across the complete spectrum of National Power,' an implicit reference to nuclear weapons.
insights.speaker: Qamar Cheema
insights.so_what: This is an explicit, on-record linkage of the India-Pakistan water dispute to nuclear escalation risk — a material tail-risk factor for South Asia geopolitical risk premia.
insights.quote: Any attempt to stop or divert the flow of water belonging to Pakistan … will be considered as an Act of War and responded with full force across the complete spectrum of National Power.
insights.locator: Pakistan's National Security Committee explicitly and bluntly warned

insights.claim: The Indus Waters Treaty dispute between India and Pakistan is emerging as a new potential trigger for conflict between the two nuclear-armed rivals, echoing a 1995 World Bank warning that future wars would be fought over water.
insights.speaker: Qamar Cheema
insights.so_what: Investors and risk analysts covering South Asia should treat water-sharing disputes as a live geopolitical risk channel alongside Kashmir and border tensions.
insights.quote: the wars of the next century will be fought over water
insights.locator: In a speech at the Stockholm Water Symposium in August 1995

insights.claim: In recent months Pakistan has given unusually heavy attention to the Indus Waters Treaty through a steady stream of official statements and extensive media coverage, signaling how much weight Islamabad now places on the water dispute with India.
insights.speaker: Qamar Cheema
insights.so_what: A sustained rise in official rhetoric is itself a leading indicator of an escalating bilateral dispute, worth tracking for further diplomatic or military signaling.
insights.locator: Pakistan has given unusual attention to the Indus Waters Treaty
